title: orchis morio subsp. picta
intro: Orchis morio subsp. picta is a sub-species of the Orchidaceae family, specifically a type of orchid commonly known as the lady's slipper orchid, characterized by its distinctive slipper-shaped flowers and vibrant markings. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and treating skin conditions. The health benefits of Orchis morio subsp. picta include its ability to improve circulation, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as an infusion, or made into a tincture, which can be taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
